storyline:
SPY is the benchmark / safe-haven, not a gem — the events the curator tracked are below.
Co-discovered events the curator juggled (not this dashboard's gem):
  • ev1 · AEM, GFI, HMY — catalyst: gains on gold price surge. Exit: still live — none - gold hit fresh record highs again this week on tariff/inflation safe-haven demand.
  • ev2 · CEG — catalyst: $840M emissions-free power deal. Exit: still live — none - original deal closed but structural nuclear/AI-power demand driver remains unresolved and ongoing.
  • ev3 · ZANGGE MINING, ZIJMY — catalyst: China lithium acquisition. Exit: still live — none - deal remains unsigned/uncollapsed per last confirmed reporting; no reversal found.
  • ev4 · GSIT — catalyst: U.S. Army AI contract secured. Exit: still live — Ten weeks with zero GSIT-specific follow-on news; contract remains unconfirmed by execution/revenue.
  • ev5 · AGNICO EAGLE MINES, DISCOVERY SILVER, GLENCORE, NEM — catalyst: Trump tariff threats. Exit: still live — none — auto tariffs and 'Liberation Day' reciprocal tariffs still ahead, driver escalating not resolved.
  • ev6 · EOLYY — catalyst: Early FDA approval. Exit: ✕ EXITED 2025-02-14 — FDA approval already announced this week — the specific binary event has occurred and is public.
  • ev7 · HLYA, ROK, XYL — catalyst: $100M share repurchase program. Exit: still live — none.
  • ev8 · CORZ — catalyst: $1.2B data center expansion. Exit: still live — CoreWeave IPO priced, shifting narrative away from original CORZ-CoreWeave deal to IPO aftermath.

AEM.assessment: Gold remains near multi-week highs with structural tailwinds (inflation, central bank buying, rate-cut expectations) supporting miners. This is a structural, open-ended commodity driver, not a single datable event, so thesis stays live absent a reversal in gold's trend.

Structural driver (tariff war, inflation, Fed uncertainty, central bank buying) remains fully intact with no reversal signal. Basket unchanged; conviction stays high given fresh milestone (new all-time high) and continued bullish sell-side commentary rather than fade/exhaustion framing.
